alert_text: High-confidence phishing email detected. Sender 'hr-support@micros0ft-secure.com' delivered a message to 23 employees with subject: 'URGENT: Password Expiry Notice — Action Required Within 24 Hours'. The embedded hyperlink (http://bit.ly/ms-reset-pwd) resolves to 185.220.101.47, a Tor exit node flagged by threat intelligence. Eleven recipients clicked the link within 12 minutes of delivery.
raw_log: 2024-03-15T08:23:41Z SMTP INBOUND from=hr-support@micros0ft-secure.com to=23_recipients subject='URGENT: Password Expiry Notice' link=http://bit.ly/ms-reset-pwd link_resolved=185.220.101.47 clicks=11 spf=FAIL dkim=FAIL dmarc=FAIL
ground_truth_containment: Block sender domain micros0ft-secure.com at email gateway
Quarantine all 23 copies of the email
Force password reset for 11 users who clicked the link
Enable MFA on all affected accounts
Block IP 185.220.101.47 at perimeter firewall
Alert IT helpdesk to watch for suspicious account activity

alert_text: Business Email Compromise (BEC) alert. A spoofed email impersonating CFO David Harrington (d.harrington@companydomain.com) was sent from 'd.harrington@company-domain.co' to the Accounts Payable team. The email instructs AP staff to process a wire transfer of $247,000 to a new vendor account (First National Bank routing 021000021, account 8844129003). This is the third BEC attempt this quarter.
raw_log: 2024-04-02T14:07:19Z SMTP INBOUND from=d.harrington@company-domain.co to=ap_team@companydomain.com subject='Wire Transfer - Vendor Payment' body_contains_banking=true display_name_mismatch=true spf=FAIL
ground_truth_containment: Block spoofed sender domain company-domain.co immediately
Contact CFO directly via phone to confirm email is fraudulent
Halt any pending wire transfer requests from this email
Brief AP team on BEC attack pattern
Review email gateway DMARC policy enforcement
File report with FBI IC3 if transfer was made

alert_text: OAuth phishing attack detected. User jane.doe@corp.com granted consent to a malicious third-party app named 'Microsoft 365 Security Update' (App ID: a3f8b1c2-9d4e-4f7a-b6c8-12d3e4f5a6b7) via an Azure AD OAuth consent page. The app requested Mail.ReadWrite, Contacts.Read, and Files.ReadWrite.All permissions. The app is not registered by Microsoft and its publisher is unverified.
raw_log: 2024-05-10T11:33:55Z AAD OAUTH_CONSENT user=jane.doe@corp.com app_id=a3f8b1c2-9d4e-4f7a-b6c8-12d3e4f5a6b7 app_name='Microsoft 365 Security Update' permissions=['Mail.ReadWrite','Contacts.Read','Files.ReadWrite.All'] publisher_verified=false
ground_truth_containment: Immediately revoke consent for app ID a3f8b1c2-9d4e-4f7a-b6c8-12d3e4f5a6b7
Audit jane.doe's mailbox for suspicious email forwarding rules
Review OneDrive/SharePoint access logs for unauthorized downloads
Force revoke all active sessions for jane.doe
Enable Conditional Access policy requiring compliant devices for admin consent
Report app to Microsoft via partner portal

alert_text: Cobalt Strike beacon activity detected on WKSTN-MRKT-007. Process 'svchost.exe' (PID 4892) spawned by 'winword.exe' is making periodic HTTP POST requests to 45.33.32.156:443 at 60-second intervals with jitter. Memory scan reveals a reflectively loaded DLL matching Cobalt Strike malleable C2 profile 'jquery-c2.4.3'. Parent document: 'Q1_Marketing_Budget_Draft.docx' received via email 4 hours ago.
raw_log: 2024-06-18T03:42:11Z EDR PROCESS_INJECTION pid=4892 name=svchost.exe parent=winword.exe parent_pid=3204 network_dst=45.33.32.156:443 interval=60s dll_inject=reflective signature=cobalt_strike_cs4
ground_truth_containment: Immediately isolate WKSTN-MRKT-007 from the network
Kill processes winword.exe (PID 3204) and svchost.exe (PID 4892)
Block IP 45.33.32.156 at perimeter and internal firewalls
Preserve memory dump and disk image for forensics
Search for same C2 IOC across all endpoints
Quarantine 'Q1_Marketing_Budget_Draft.docx' from all email systems
Reset sarah.chen credentials and revoke active sessions
Engage IR team — assume lateral movement has occurred

alert_text: Ransomware precursor activity detected. Host FILESVR-01 shows: (1) Volume Shadow Copies deleted via 'vssadmin delete shadows /all /quiet'; (2) Windows Backup service stopped; (3) Recursive enumeration of \filesvr-01\finance_shared with 14,000 file reads in 3 minutes; (4) New executable 'update_kb5012345.exe' dropped to C:\Windows\Temp. YARA rule match: Conti_ransomware_v3.
raw_log: 2024-07-22T09:15:44Z EDR RANSOMWARE_PRECURSOR host=FILESVR-01 vss_deleted=true backup_stopped=true file_enum_count=14000 new_binary=update_kb5012345.exe yara_match=Conti_v3 severity=CRITICAL
ground_truth_containment: IMMEDIATELY isolate FILESVR-01 — do not power off (preserve evidence)
Kill process update_kb5012345.exe before encryption begins
Activate IR playbook — declare ransomware incident
Identify patient zero — trace how the executable reached FILESVR-01
Check all hosts for same YARA signature match
Notify executive team and legal counsel
Contact cyber insurance carrier
Restore from last clean backup once environment is clear

alert_text: Cryptomining malware detected on 12 corporate endpoints. Process 'conhost.exe' clones are consuming 85–95% CPU on hosts WKSTN-DEV-012 through WKSTN-DEV-023. Network traffic analysis shows outbound connections to pool.minergate.com:4444 (XMRig Monero miner). Binary hash SHA256: 7d5f6a3b2c1e9d8f4a7b6c5d3e2f1a0b matches known XMRig variant. All 12 hosts visited 'free-software-downloads.io' within 24 hours of infection.
raw_log: 2024-08-05T16:58:22Z EDR CRYPTOMINER hosts=12 process=conhost.exe cpu_avg=91% dst=pool.minergate.com:4444 hash=7d5f6a3b2c1e9d8f4a7b6c5d3e2f1a0b origin_domain=free-software-downloads.io
ground_truth_containment: Block pool.minergate.com and 104.21.47.200 at DNS and firewall
Block free-software-downloads.io at web proxy
Kill all conhost.exe processes on 12 affected hosts
Delete XMRig binary and run full AV scan
Check for persistence mechanisms (scheduled tasks, registry run keys)
Review browser history on all 12 hosts for additional IOCs
Consider reimaging if persistence is widespread

alert_text: Pass-the-Hash attack detected. SIEM correlation: User account 'corp\svc_backup' successfully authenticated to 7 hosts (WKSTN-HR-001 through WKSTN-HR-007) within 4 minutes using NTLM authentication. Source host WKSTN-IT-099 shows lsass.exe memory access by 'mimikatz.exe' (renamed as 'taskmgr.exe') 18 minutes prior. The backup service account does not normally authenticate interactively.
raw_log: 2024-09-11T02:17:33Z SIEM LATERAL_MOVEMENT src=WKSTN-IT-099 technique=pass_the_hash account=corp\svc_backup targets=7 auth_type=NTLM lsass_access=true mimikatz_detected=true renamed_binary=taskmgr.exe
ground_truth_containment: Isolate WKSTN-IT-099 immediately
Disable svc_backup account and reset its password
Force logoff of svc_backup sessions on all 7 target hosts
Run Mimikatz detection sweep across entire domain
Rotate all service account passwords discovered on WKSTN-IT-099
Enable Protected Users security group for all service accounts
Review all NTLM authentication logs for past 24 hours
Check for new local admin accounts or persistence on 7 target hosts

alert_text: Kerberoasting attack in progress. IDS detected 847 Kerberos TGS_REQ requests from single workstation WKSTN-FIN-055 targeting 63 service principal names (SPNs) within 90 seconds. Requests target high-value SPNs including MSSQLSvc, HTTP/intranet, and CIFS/filesvr. This pattern matches automated Kerberoasting tools (Rubeus, GetUserSPNs.py). User account 'john.smith' is logged onto the source workstation.
raw_log: 2024-10-03T23:44:02Z IDS KERBEROASTING src=192.168.3.55 dst_dc=192.168.1.10 tgs_req_count=847 spn_count=63 duration=90s user=john.smith tool_signature=rubeus
ground_truth_containment: Isolate WKSTN-FIN-055 immediately
Reset john.smith password and force logoff
Reset passwords for all 63 targeted service accounts
Audit all recently offline cracked service accounts
Enable 'Use AES encryption types' for service accounts
Search for Rubeus.exe or GetUserSPNs.py artifacts on WKSTN-FIN-055
Check for exfiltration of kerberoastable ticket hashes
Review if john.smith account was compromised via phishing first

alert_text: RDP lateral movement detected. Host SRV-JUMP-01 (jump server) established RDP sessions to 11 internal servers (SRV-DB-01, SRV-DB-02, SRV-APP-01 through SRV-APP-09) within 7 minutes using credentials of 'corp\administrator'. Each session lasted under 2 minutes. Jump server's usual pattern is 1–2 RDP sessions per day. Source user alert: administrator account was logged out 3 hours ago.
raw_log: 2024-11-20T18:22:14Z SIEM RDP_LATERAL src=SRV-JUMP-01 dst_count=11 account=corp\administrator session_duration_avg=110s credential_status=logged_out_3h_ago
ground_truth_containment: Lock corp\administrator account immediately
Terminate all 11 RDP sessions from SRV-JUMP-01
Isolate SRV-JUMP-01 and all 11 destination servers
Run forensics on SRV-JUMP-01 to identify attackers entry point
Review all administrator actions on 11 target servers
Check for web shells, backdoors, or new local admin accounts
Rotate domain administrator password via separate clean workstation
Implement just-in-time privileged access for jump server

alert_text: Data Loss Prevention alert: Large file transfer detected. User 'robert.kim' (Engineering) uploaded 4.7 GB of data to personal Dropbox account (account linked to robert.kim.personal@gmail.com) via the corporate network over 23 minutes. Upload contained 847 files including file extensions .py, .sql, .xlsx, .docx. robert.kim submitted resignation yesterday. DLP policy 'Block_Cloud_Personal_Storage' should have blocked this — policy enforcement is currently set to 'Audit' mode.
raw_log: 2024-12-04T14:53:07Z DLP CLOUD_UPLOAD user=robert.kim dst=dropbox.com dst_ip=162.125.248.10 size_gb=4.7 file_count=847 extensions=py,sql,xlsx,docx hr_status=resignation_submitted policy=audit_only
ground_truth_containment: Block outbound connections to Dropbox from WKSTN-ENG-087
Disable robert.kim account immediately
Preserve all forensic evidence before HR takes action
Identify exactly which files were uploaded (run inventory against uploaded list)
Assess if any uploaded files contain trade secrets or IP
Contact legal counsel — this may require litigation hold
Enable DLP policy enforcement mode (change Audit to Block)
Review if robert.kim shared credentials with any external parties

alert_text: DNS tunneling / data exfiltration detected. IDS rule triggered: host WKSTN-LEGAL-003 is generating 2,340 DNS TXT record queries per hour to subdomains of 'data-sync.analytics-cloud.net'. Subdomains are base64-encoded strings up to 62 characters long (e.g., 'aGVsbG8gd29ybGQgdGhpcyBpcyBhIHRlc3Q.data-sync.analytics-cloud.net'). DNS traffic to this domain began 6 hours ago. The domain was registered 3 days ago. No legitimate business use of this domain.
raw_log: 2024-12-18T01:28:44Z IDS DNS_TUNNEL src=WKSTN-LEGAL-003 dns_server=8.8.8.8 queries_per_hour=2340 query_type=TXT domain=data-sync.analytics-cloud.net encoding=base64 domain_age_days=3
ground_truth_containment: Block domain data-sync.analytics-cloud.net at DNS resolver and firewall
Isolate WKSTN-LEGAL-003 immediately
Capture all DNS query content to decode what was exfiltrated
Reverse-engineer the tunneling tool on the endpoint
Determine data sensitivity based on decoded DNS payloads
Check for process responsible for DNS queries and kill it
Audit patricia.morgan's account for signs of compromise or insider threat
Review registrar contact info for analytics-cloud.net for attribution

alert_text: Suspicious email forwarding rule detected. EDR and Exchange monitoring identified a new inbox rule created by 'alex.nguyen@corp.com': Rule name '_' (blank), condition: forward all emails matching keywords ['invoice', 'payment', 'wire', 'bank', 'confidential', 'contract'] to external address 'alex.nguyen.work@protonmail.com'. Rule was created at 19:47 from IP 77.88.55.66 (Russia, Yandex ISP) — alex.nguyen's usual location is San Francisco, CA.
raw_log: 2025-01-09T20:14:33Z EXCHANGE FORWARDING_RULE user=alex.nguyen@corp.com rule_name=_ keywords=invoice,payment,wire,bank,confidential,contract external_dst=alex.nguyen.work@protonmail.com creation_ip=77.88.55.66 creation_location=RU:Yandex
ground_truth_containment: Delete the malicious forwarding rule immediately
Disable alex.nguyen account and revoke all active sessions
Force password reset for alex.nguyen
Review all emails that have been forwarded since rule creation
Contact protonmail abuse team to request email interception
Investigate whether 77.88.55.66 was used for other corp accounts
Enable impossible travel alerts in Azure AD / Okta
Brief legal team on scope of potentially exfiltrated emails

alert_text: IDS Alert: Port scan detected from 10.0.50.15 targeting 254 internal hosts on ports 22, 80, 443, 3389, 8080 over 15 minutes. Total of 3,048 connection attempts recorded.
raw_log: 2024-06-12T10:41:22Z IDS PORTSCAN src=10.0.50.15 dst_range=10.0.0.0/24 ports=[22,80,443,3389,8080] attempts=3048 duration=15m src_hostname=SEC-TOOLING-01
ground_truth_containment: No action required — this is authorized Nessus vulnerability scanning from the security team.

alert_text: DLP Alert: Large file transfer detected. 18 GB uploaded to AWS S3 bucket 'corp-backups-us-east-1' from server BKUP-SRV-01 (IP 192.168.20.5). Transfer occurred between 22:00 and 23:15 UTC.
raw_log: 2024-08-28T22:05:51Z DLP CLOUD_UPLOAD user=svc_backup dst=s3.amazonaws.com bucket=corp-backups-us-east-1 size_gb=18 dst_ip=52.217.128.0 schedule=nightly_2200
ground_truth_containment: No action required — this is the scheduled nightly backup job running as expected.

alert_text: EDR Alert: PowerShell script execution with -EncodedCommand flag detected on WKSTN-OPS-044. Base64-encoded payload executed by user 'mike.operations'. Process spawned child processes curl.exe and robocopy.exe.
raw_log: 2024-09-05T14:22:37Z EDR POWERSHELL host=WKSTN-OPS-044 user=mike.operations cmdline='powershell.exe -EncodedCommand UwB0AGEAcgB0AC0AUwBsAGUAZQBwACAALQBTAGUAYwBvAG4AZABzACAAMQA=' children=[curl.exe,robocopy.exe] signed_script=true admin_approved=true
ground_truth_containment: No action required — this is an IT operations automation script, whitelisted in EDR policy.

alert_text: SIEM Alert: Multiple failed SSH login attempts to 15 servers from IP 10.40.0.100, followed by successful logins using public key authentication. 342 failed attempts over 8 minutes before success.
raw_log: 2024-10-14T09:19:04Z SIEM SSH_BRUTEFORCE src=DEPLOY-RUNNER-01 failed_attempts=342 targets=15 auth_method=pubkey src_hostname=DEPLOY-RUNNER-01 pipeline_id=ci-deploy-prod-v2.3.1
ground_truth_containment: No action required — this is a CI/CD deployment pipeline. The 'failed attempts' are host key verification checks, not brute force.

alert_text: Email gateway alert: Bulk email campaign flagged as potential spam. 45,000 emails sent from corp.com marketing domain to external recipients over 3 hours. Subject: 'Exclusive Black Friday Offer — 30% Off All Products'. SPF and DKIM pass. Bounce rate: 2.3%.
raw_log: 2024-11-22T15:44:18Z SMTP OUTBOUND count=45000 from=noreply@corp.com subject='Exclusive Black Friday Offer' spf=PASS dkim=PASS bounce_rate=2.3% origin=mailchimp_integration campaign_id=BF2024
ground_truth_containment: No action required — this is the authorized Black Friday marketing email campaign via Mailchimp.

alert_text: EDR Alert: Antivirus detected and quarantined 'eicar.txt' on WKSTN-IT-031. File contained the EICAR test string. Process responsible: 'chrome.exe'. URL accessed: 'https://www.eicar.org/download/eicar.com.txt'.
raw_log: 2024-12-03T11:27:59Z AV QUARANTINE host=WKSTN-IT-031 file=eicar.txt hash=275a021bbfb6489e54d471899f7db9d1663fc695ec2fe2a2c4538aabf651fd0f url=https://eicar.org/download/eicar.com.txt process=chrome.exe action=quarantined
ground_truth_containment: No action required — this is the EICAR antivirus test file used for AV functionality testing. david.bishop should be informed that AV is working correctly.

alert_text: PowerShell Empire C2 beacon detected. WKSTN-EXEC-002 shows obfuscated PowerShell executing from registry run key 'HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\Updater'. Decoded payload establishes an encrypted communications channel to 91.92.128.77:8080 using HTTP GET/POST. IDS correlation: matches PowerShell Empire default RC4 encrypted staging traffic pattern.
raw_log: 2025-01-15T07:33:21Z EDR POWERSHELL_EMPIRE host=WKSTN-EXEC-002 user=ceo_account persistence=registry_run dst=91.92.128.77:8080 encryption=RC4 signature=empire_default_stager
ground_truth_containment: CRITICAL: This is the CEO's workstation — escalate to CISO immediately
Isolate WKSTN-EXEC-002 without alerting the user (risk of evidence destruction)
Remove registry persistence key before any reboot
Block 91.92.128.77 at all network egress points
Forensically image the system before remediation
Check CEO email, calendar, and OneDrive for data exfiltration
Determine initial vector — likely spear-phishing or watering hole
Activate full IR team — C-suite compromise is a critical incident

alert_text: Firewall anomaly: WKSTN-ACCT-011 has established 87 outbound HTTPS connections to 23 distinct IP addresses in Ukraine, Russia, and Belarus since midnight — 0 such connections in the previous 30 days. Total outbound data: 2.1 GB. No corresponding inbound traffic. User 'finance.analyst3' is not scheduled to work overnight. Corporate proxy bypassed (connections go direct).
raw_log: 2025-02-03T04:12:38Z FW OUTBOUND_ANOMALY src=WKSTN-ACCT-011 user=finance.analyst3 dst_count=23 geo=UA,RU,BY connections=87 data_out_gb=2.1 proxy_bypass=true shift_hours=false
ground_truth_containment: Isolate WKSTN-ACCT-011 immediately via network quarantine
Preserve firewall logs showing all 87 connection destinations
Begin forensic analysis to identify the malware initiating connections
Review what financial data finance.analyst3 had access to
Reset finance.analyst3 credentials across all systems
Contact finance.analyst3 to determine if they were working remotely
Alert CISO and legal team — financial data may have been exfiltrated
Submit IOCs to threat intel platform for correlation

alert_text: Credential phishing via macro-enabled document. User 'tom.bradley' opened 'Invoice_Feb2025.xlsm' from email attachment. Excel spawned cmd.exe which executed mshta.exe to download and run a VBScript from 'http://update-helper.xyz/payload.vbs'. The script enumerates credentials stored in Chrome and Firefox password managers and attempts to POST them to 'http://update-helper.xyz/collect'.
raw_log: 2025-02-18T09:55:14Z EDR MACRO_EXEC host=WKSTN-SALES-088 user=tom.bradley file=Invoice_Feb2025.xlsm child=mshta.exe payload_url=http://update-helper.xyz/payload.vbs credential_dump=browser_passwords exfil_dst=http://update-helper.xyz/collect
ground_truth_containment: Immediately block update-helper.xyz and 185.234.219.30
Kill mshta.exe and cmd.exe processes on WKSTN-SALES-088
Determine if credential exfiltration POST request completed
Reset all of tom.bradley's browser-saved passwords
Force password reset on all corporate applications tom.bradley used
Enable MFA on all tom.bradley's accounts
Quarantine Invoice_Feb2025.xlsm and report to email gateway IOCs
Check if payload was downloaded on other hosts from same email campaign

alert_text: SIEM Alert: Repeated failed authentications for domain administrator account. 156 failed logins over 12 minutes from host PENTEST-KALI-01 to multiple servers. Followed by successful logins.
raw_log: 2025-03-01T16:30:44Z SIEM BRUTE_FORCE src=PENTEST-KALI-01 failed=156 duration=12m auth_success=true pentest_ticket=PT-2025-0301 authorized_by=CISO
ground_truth_containment: No action required — this is an authorized red team penetration test (ticket PT-2025-0301, approved by CISO). Coordinate with red team for scoping.
